game_name: Leisure Suit Larry in the Land of the Lounge Lizards

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate Lost Wages, acquire money through gambling, interact with NPCs, gift-giving to advance relationships, solve puzzles to access new areas and reach the final goal
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure (7 real-time hours to complete before game over) combined with resource scarcity (limited starting money) and relationship progression gates
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Escapist wish-fulfillment as a social underdog pursuing romantic/sexual conquest in a resort city; subversive humor through double-entendres and absurdist scenarios
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unapologetically adult-oriented comedy adventure with explicit sexual content; rejection of moral gatekeeping (players can contract STD if unprotected; condom purchase is optional puzzle solution). Campy tone treats serious themes (virginity, loneliness, mortality) with irreverent humor rather than judgment. The automatic revival sequence (bodies reassembled in lab) subverts death stakes with meta-humor referencing other Sierra games.

difficulty.lives_system: Soft death system: early deaths trigger automatic revival via laboratory reassembly sequence (original) or blender reconstruction (1991 remake); player continues without penalty beyond lost time
difficulty.continues_system: Infinite continues via auto-revival mechanic; no traditional game over until time limit expires (7 hours)
difficulty.checkpointing: Death-based checkpointing: lethal mistakes reset player to current location with time/resources preserved; only hard time limit (7 hours) causes permanent failure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Time management is player-controlled through pacing decisions; gambling provides optional difficulty adjustment (successful casino play extends resource availability); condom purchase gates access to consequence-free progression path

progression.upgrade_system: Resource acquisition through gambling (blackjack, slots) to fund taxi travel and gift purchases; gifts unlock NPC relationship progression gates; condom purchase removes STD death penalty
progression.unlock_mechanism: Geographical gating via taxi cost system (money required to access new areas); relationship gating via gift requirements to advance with key NPCs; narrative gate via condom purchase enabling safe progression
replayability_hooks: Multiple gift pathways to achieve same NPC relationship outcomes
Different gambling outcomes create variable resource availability
Optional STD consequence path for players willing to experience alternate failure state
Text parser allows multiple valid command phrasings for discovery-based gameplay

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling: Lost Wages as parody of Las Vegas with thematic locations (bar, casino, wedding chapel, disco, convenience store)
NPC portraiture: detailed character images for key romance targets distinguish them from background NPCs and humanize relationship progression
Dialogue-driven characterization: text parser interactions reveal NPC personalities through response variation
Meta-narrative through auto-revival sequence: references to other Sierra games (King's Quest) break fourth wall and establish shared universe
Contextual humor: double-entendres and campy dialogue establish tone without explicit exposition

faction_indicators: Sexual libertinism vs. monogamy (Faith's boyfriend, Eve as ultimate goal)
Class markers via aesthetics (1970s leisure suit as dated/low-status attire)
Respectability signaling (sex worker as lowest-status option; receptionist as higher-status target)
danger_signals: STD mechanic: health consequences for unprotected sex with sex worker signals disease transmission risk
Time pressure induces decision-making under duress
Mugging/traffic death in areas without taxi payment reflects urban crime/chaos anxiety
environmental_storytelling: Lost Wages setting evokes escapism and economic gambling (literal and social)
Bar/casino/wedding chapel/disco venues establish hedonistic social hierarchy
Basement dwelling origin story grounds character in social failure/isolation
Convenience store condom availability normalizes safe-sex logistics within game economy
